response.justification: The candidate is an experienced, versatile engineer, but their profile does not match this role's core requirements. Professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, far short of the mandated 5–10 years of hands-on Windows C++ development. Their financial-sector exposure (RIDE Capital) was Node.js/TypeScript-based fintech work, not Fixed Income or Capital Markets systems, and there is no evidence of SQL Server or Oracle depth. Strengths in D, TypeScript, and web platforms do not offset the missing mandatory skills.
strengths: Nearly 20 years of broad software engineering experience across web, backend, and embedded systems
Strong low-latency and systems programming background (DLang async I/O at Dunnhumby, C/C++ at National Instruments)
Solid engineering practices: TDD, CI/CD, DevOps tooling, and open-source contributions
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, versus the required 5–10 years on Windows
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; fintech exposure was TypeScript-based
No demonstrated SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL

key_factors.factor: Missing capital markets / fixed income domain expertise
Strong general engineering and systems programming fundamentals
